response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior full-stack engineer with strong craftsmanship credentials (TDD, open-source, teaching), but the profile diverges substantially from this role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. Large-scale infrastructure exposure is real but brief (~2 years of low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, mostly in DLang), and there is no evidence of formal technical leadership of a cloud compute platform or of mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers in a lead capacity.
strengths: High-throughput, low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering-practice foundation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community teaching/workshops
Broad polyglot background with some cloud exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement; primary systems language is DLang, not C++
No formal technical-lead role owning platform strategy, priorities, or cross-team alignment
Career centered on web/product full-stack work rather than cloud compute platforms or distributed infrastructure at scale
